title: Reduced intellectual development in children with prenatal lead exposure.
ehr_evidence: The study used direct measurements of blood lead (BPb) and psychometric tests in a prospective cohort, not electronic health records.
summary: A prospective cohort study in Mexico City investigating the effects of prenatal and postnatal lead exposure on child intellectual development.
key_findings: Prenatal lead exposure is associated with reduced intellectual development in children.
population: 321 children born in Mexico City (175 followed after 5 years) between 1987 and 1992
limitations: Small sample size of children tested after 5 years
